title: EVs’ Year Of Living Dangerously
text:
EVs’ Year Of Living Dangerously

AJ Cortese, Ernan Cui

Gavekal Technologies: New Energy | 21 Jul 2026

China’s passenger car market is having a terrible 2026, and electric vehicles for once are no exception. Domestic EV sales are on track to fall by -7% this year, their first-ever annual decline. Prices and margins are under pressure. Yet despite all that, the market is not consolidating—quite the opposite. Long-time market leaders BYD and Tesla are losing share to at least a half-dozen competitors with strong technology, good designs and vertically integrated supply chains. What makes it all possible is exports, which now account for a third of Chinese EV makers’ unit sales. The question is how long international markets will be willing to absorb the rising tide of Chinese imports.China’s EV market will shrink in 2026 for the first time. But thanks in part to booming exports, the industry is avoiding its long-awaited consolidation.
